body:
### distinct failure
The "File", "Search", and "Help" items in the window toolbar/menu bar have bounding boxes that are too tight. When a user hovers over them, the `win95-beveled` effect appears, which causes the text inside to be slightly truncated/clipped due to insufficient padding (`px-1`). 

The user also requested that these clickable areas be visible at all times, not just on hover, to provide better clarity on where to click.

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Open any window in the portfolio (e.g., "Welcome.txt" or "My Portfolio").
2. Hover over the "File", "Search", or "Help" labels in the toolbar at the top of the window.
3. Observe the `win95-beveled` effect appearing and the text within being slightly clipped or truncated.

### Expected Behavior
1. The bounding box (padding) for the toolbar items should be increased to ensure that the bevel effect does not truncate the text.
2. The bounding box/bevel should be visible at all times, making it obvious that these are interactive buttons.

### Environment
- OS: Windows 95 Portfolio Website
- Browser: All browsers (Visual Styling Issue)

### Technical Note
This issue is likely located in `components/win95/Win95Window.tsx` within the Menu Bar section (lines 449-506), where the `hover:win95-beveled` class and `px-1` padding are used.
title: [Bug]: Window toolbar icons (File, Search, Help) have tight bounding boxes and truncate text
